steps.text: Let $u = x^2$ to turn the quartic into a quadratic: $x^4 - x^2 - 6$ becomes $u^2 - u - 6$.
Factor the quadratic $u^2 - u - 6$. Find two numbers that multiply to $-6$ and add to $-1$: those numbers are $+2$ and $-3$. So $u^2 - u - 6 = (u + 2)(u - 3)$.
Verify: $(x^2 + 2)(x^2 - 3) = x^4 - 3x^2 + 2x^2 - 6 = x^4 - x^2 - 6$ ✓. That matches choice B — B is correct.

choices.text: • $(x^2+1)(x^2-6) = x^4 - 6x^2 + x^2 - 6 = x^4 - 5x^2 - 6$. The middle term is $-5x^2$, not $-x^2$. Eliminated.
• $(x^2+2)(x^2-3) = x^4 - 3x^2 + 2x^2 - 6 = x^4 - x^2 - 6$ ✓. That matches choice B — B is correct.
• $(x^2+3)(x^2-2) = x^4 - 2x^2 + 3x^2 - 6 = x^4 + x^2 - 6$. The middle term is $+x^2$, not $-x^2$. Eliminated.
• $(x^2+6)(x^2-1) = x^4 - x^2 + 6x^2 - 6 = x^4 + 5x^2 - 6$. The middle term is $+5x^2$, not $-x^2$. Eliminated.
